text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. One key principle is responsive design, which ensures the app looks good and works smoothly on various screen sizes and orientations. Developers must choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific operating system like iOS or Android, offering excellent performance and full access to device features. However, developing separately for each platform can be time-consuming. Cross-platform tools like React Native allow writing code once for multiple systems, but performance may sometimes suffer. After development, apps are published on app stores like Google Play and the Apple App Store. These stores have strict review processes and rules that developers must follow. Finally, performance is crucial—users expect fast loading times, smooth animations, and minimal battery usage. Poor performance can lead to negative reviews and uninstalls.
